title: P5.2 — Decide determinism tolerance and exception policy for C-09
description: Approve the determinism acceptance boundary and exception mechanism. This is the G2 owner disposition input; G4 implements the failing comparison and approved-exception check. (§5 C-09; §8 P5; RCON-10)

comments.content: P5.2 DRAFTED as PROPOSED — PR opened, NOT merged, awaiting owner batch-ratification. Disposes C-09: (1) a FAILING determinism acceptance gate additive to the non-gating determinism-probe.yml — compares canonical parse-dump projection over cross_os_comparable cells, runs on all parse-perturbing surfaces (R/**, DESCRIPTION, NAMESPACE, tools/determinism/**, dep bumps) + schedule + as pre-graduation gate for curl-free 3.0; DEGRADED cell = failure. (2) Tolerance = byte-exact ZERO unapproved divergence (no fuzzy threshold; frozen 2026-07-20 sweep already achieves single-MD5). (3) Append-only determinism-exceptions register (id/owner/justification/scope/signature/expiry/tracking/state) consumed by the gate; new/uncovered divergence fails. Mirrors P5.3 ledger; cache→P5.1, engine→P0.4. Grounded in S8, §10.

title: P5 — Operational contract owner decisions (§8)
description:
Owner-decision tier for performance/memory budgets, cache semantics, curl-removal closure, oracle policy, determinism tolerance, migration window, and graduation gates.

Stable contradiction consumers: C-08 and C-09; C-12 also consumes oracle policy. C-10 remains a P0 release-boundary decision, with P5 only supplying downstream operational criteria.

Create atomic decision leaves under the G0.3 schema; accepted tracker status alone is non-normative. (§8 P5; RCON-09–RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G0.3 — Record schemas + lifecycle state machine (§6 authority records + transition table)
description:
§6 artifact 2 + lifecycle. Parent task for G0.3a common artifact envelope/validator and G0.3b invalidation/re-open semantics.

Define tracked schemas for immutable owner decisions, accepted designs, and source-claim, decision, contradiction, and finding registers. Encode all ten states and allowed transitions with actor, timestamp, evidence, and dependency IDs.

Import/validate P0.1 without rewriting substance; enforce append-only accepted decisions, single writers, referential integrity, and non-authoritative tracker state.

Done only when both child validators pass and the schemas can drive later leaf slicing. (§4 RCON-01; §6; §7 G0)

P0.1-P0.3 import & validate unchanged. Both child validators pass (see G0.3a/G0.3b).

title: Epic: rurl 3.0 protocol hardening — amend & re-review before product-spec draft
description:
Scope: §11 next frontier + §7 pre-draft gates. Product-spec drafting remains out of scope until durable G5 acceptance.

Two interlocking structures:
- gate acceptance order G0→G1→G2→G3→G4→G5;
- owner-decision tiers P0→P5, consumed through atomic durable records rather than tracker status.

PHASING: schema-dependent leaf fan-out remains deferred until G0.3 and P0.3. Stable work is instantiated now: four atomic P0 decisions; P1–P5 containers; known contradiction decision leaves; C-01–C-12 dispositions; S1–S9 evidence transfer; artifact-wide validator/invalidation semantics; artifact-4 G1→G3 handoff; semantic cache closure; C-08/C-09/C-10 G4 verification; and dual G5 review.

DURABILITY: authoritative manifest/registers/decisions/findings and ten-state lifecycle are tracked, schema-validated records. Accepted records are append-only; tracker-only decisions have no authority. The manifest separates source baseline 89be90b from the later frozen control-plane snapshot and hashes a tracker export.

SEQUENCING: G2 resolves every contradiction before G3; G4 verifies already-approved policy. Any invalidated upstream record fails/reopens downstream acceptance.

Depends on RURL-jairsufw. Placement remains provisional until P0.3 records final topology before snapshot freeze. (§1, §4–§11; RCON-01–RCON-10; C-01–C-12)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

comments.author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com
comments.content: Independent roadmap review fixes applied: split P0 and removed C-10 from G0 prerequisites; instantiated P1–P5 and contradiction-specific owner decisions; added C-01–C-12 dispositions with corrected cross-tier wiring; added evidence transfer, universal artifact validation, invalidation/re-open semantics, artifact-4 handoff, semantic cache closure, G2 acceptance, G4 verification-only leaves, and durable G5 review fan-in. Remaining schema-shaped leaf expansion stays deferred until G0.3/P0.3.

comments.author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com
comments.content:
Authoritative design docs governing this epic's product decisions (consult BEFORE re-opening any posture/behavior question):

- design/prd/browser-fixer-and-url-search-classifier.md — the posture model (Accepted, graduated per ADR 0008). Settles: http-prepend = single scheme_policy=infer seam (ADR 0012 D4); 'browser' is the fix-up/repair posture that reaches it (fixer step 4); strict = seam off, scheme-less rejects; credentials preserved by design (line 172).
- design/adr/0012-general-url-parser-scope.md — general parser scope, scheme admission, D4 one-prepend seam, D6 profiles.
- design/adr/0010-scheme-policy-acceptance-axis.md — scheme_policy / scheme_acceptance axes.
- design/work/url-v3/protocol-review-reconciliation.md — the reconciliation authority (RCON, C-01..C-12, gates G0-G5, P0-P5 queue).

Owner directive (2026-07-23): merge the P-tier decision PRs 'in the shape defined in the PRD'; when unsure what to build, look up these documents rather than asking questions the PRD already answers.

title: P5.1 — Decide authoritative cache contract for C-08
description: Choose the authoritative cache default and semantic-transparency/documentation policy. This is the G2 owner disposition input; G3 closes the semantic contract and G4 implements the documentation-consistency gate. (§5 C-08; §8 P5; RCON-09)

comments.content: P5.1 DRAFTED as PROPOSED — PR opened, NOT merged, awaiting owner batch-ratification. v3 cache contract: 3 caches (full_parse bounded-100k/reset-watermark, puny_encode/decode unbounded-by-design), semantic-transparency invariant (byte-identical results across cold/warm/enabled/disabled/bounded/evicted/scalar-vector-chunked; only time+memory differ) — the invariant P5.3's oracle checks. C-08 = code-authoritative: 100k runtime default is truth (memory-safety; unbounded = OOM/DoS risk; man/help already correct), stale README corrected (deferred to G4-tier impl task), + documentation-consistency gate that fails on runtime-vs-docs divergence → G4. Grounded in S8, §10, R/zzz.R:81.

Claim policy: conformance asserted only against P2.2's FSSS full-string (+ parse->serialize->parse identity), never the 18-field projection; historical projection claims preserved as evidence, retired as oracles. 5-authority labeled oracle taxonomy (whatwg-wpt/rfc3986-grammar/libcurl-parity/browser-parity/self-metamorphic); parity never certifies conformance; headline = 158/99 labeled count not 100% probe. Positive/negative coverage across 8 G4 families. Defers determinism->P5.2, cache/perf->P5.1, release->P0.4. 5 open questions.

- G4.3 release-rule verification verifies C-10.

Remaining schema-shaped leaves: fixture/oracle catalog; metamorphic properties; claim traceability; benchmark/memory budgets; curl zero-reference gate; full-string/state/vector/mutation/join/migration coverage; exact CI commands.

Artifact 11 receives the same §6 schema/owner/lifecycle/location/completion contract. (§6 artifact 11; §7 G4; RCON-09, RCON-10; C-08–C-10)

title: G5 — Independent re-review & acceptance (§7 G5)
description:
§7 G5. Depends on complete G4 evidence.

Instantiated leaves:
- context-free comprehensibility/completeness review;
- independent evidence-aware citation/executable-claim review;
- findings closure and final acceptance fan-in.

Both reviews bind exact protocol and manifest hashes. Every finding uses the durable lifecycle. Any material normative/source change invalidates both reviews and reopens final acceptance.

Only accepted G5.3 with objective yes/no evidence may unblock a successor product-spec draft. (§6 lifecycle; §7 G5; RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: C-12 — Retire projection-based conformance claims and define rebuild boundary

title: C-09 — Dispose evidence-only determinism workflow contradiction

title: G5.3 — Review-finding closure and final protocol acceptance
description:
Fan-in for G5 acceptance.

Dispose every finding from both independent reviews in the durable finding register; verify closure state and objective yes/no evidence for every amended-protocol acceptance question; require the accepted protocol hash to equal the reviewed hash.

Any material normative change invalidates both reviews and reopens this acceptance. Only this accepted durable gate record may unblock a successor product-spec draft. (§6 lifecycle; §7 G5; RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

Why: this is release authority, not a durability prerequisite.

Done: a typed, durable owner decision records authority, rationale, affected claims/releases, and invalidated artifacts. G2 consumes the disposition; G4 verifies the resulting release rule. (§5 C-10; §8 P0; RCON-10)

title: G1 — Baseline & inventory closure (§7 G1) [remaining leaves deferred]
description:
§7 G1 + inventory obligations from RCON-05/RCON-09. Depends on the objective G0.4 restore drill.

Instantiated stable leaves:
- G1.1 transfers S1–S9 detailed evidence with forward/inverse coverage checks;

title: Cross-validate v3 P-tier decision records against the governing PRD/ADRs (drift audit) — blocks P-tier merge
description:
## Goal

Cross-validate every v3 P-tier owner-decision record (and the registers they
touch) against the **governing PRD/ADR docs** to catch and fix any place where a
record drifted from — contradicts, silently narrows, or re-opens — a question the
design docs already settled. Blocking: the P-tier PRs (#209–#216, 11 records)
must NOT be owner-merged (= irreversible acceptance) until this audit is clean or
every divergence is an owner-approved intentional supersession.

## Why

A near-miss surfaced at ratification: the agent reasoned from the decision
records instead of the governing PRD and manufactured a false decision fork
(`compatibility → infer`) that the PRD's shared-seam model (http-prepend =
single `scheme_policy=infer` seam, ADR 0012 D4; `browser` = the repair posture
that reaches it) had already closed. Owner: "very worried we created things that
drift from the PRD."

## Oracle docs (authority order)

1. `design/prd/browser-fixer-and-url-search-classifier.md` (posture model;
   http-prepend seam; strict = reject scheme-less; credentials preserved by
   design, line 172)
2. `design/adr/0012-general-url-parser-scope.md` (D1–D7: parser scope, admission,
   one-prepend seam D4, profiles D6, mailto D7)
3. `design/adr/0010-scheme-policy-acceptance-axis.md`, `0011`, `0007`, `0004`,
   `0003`, `0006`
4. `design/prd/url-standard-selector*.md`, `host-validation-policy.md`,
   `email-userinfo-diagnostics.md`
5. `design/work/url-v3/protocol-review-reconciliation.md` (RCON-01..10,
   C-01..12, gates, P0–P5 queue)

## Scope — records to audit (11)

P0.4 (#209) · P1.1 + P2.3 (#210) · P2.1 (#211) · P2.2 + P5.3 (#212) ·
P2.4 + P4.1 (#213) · P3.1 (#214) · P5.1 (#215) · P5.2 (#216), plus the register
rows each cites (contradictions.md C-01..12, public-surface-inventory,
source-claims, findings).

## Method

For each record: extract its normative claims/dispositions; trace each to a
specific PRD/ADR/reconciliation line that supports it, OR flag it as one of:
- **DRIFT-CONTRADICT** — asserts something the docs decide otherwise;
- **DRIFT-NARROW/WIDEN** — silently changes the scope of a settled rule;
- **DRIFT-REOPEN** — re-litigates a closed question;
- **UNGROUNDED** — a normative claim with no doc backing (may be legitimate new
  policy, but must be named as such, not smuggled in).

Priority focus (highest drift risk): the posture/prepend model in P2.1/P2.4 vs
the browser-fixer PRD; the credential/serializer surfaces in P2.2/P4.1 vs PRD
line 172 + ADR 0012; the admission lattice in P2.4 vs ADR 0012 D3/D4/0004.

## Acceptance

A drift ledger (one row per record: claim → oracle line → verdict). Zero
unresolved DRIFT-* rows, or each remaining one carries an explicit owner
"intentional supersession" note. Only then may the P-tier PRs be merged.
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

comments.author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com
comments.content:
DRIFT AUDIT COMPLETE (3 parallel passes, all 11 records traced to PRD/ADR/reconciliation + code-verified). Result: records substantially faithful; ONE structural governance drift + 5 minor fixes. No UNGROUNDED normative claims beyond the ones listed; no code-anchored fact wrong.

MUST-FIX before merge:
1. [HEADLINE] Strict-default flip contradicts accepted ADRs. P2.1/P2.4 make v3 default strict (scheme-less rejects) but carry supersedes:[] while ADR 0010 fixes 'infer (default), byte-for-byte' and ADR 0012 D4 fixes infer/byte-identity as a 'hard CRAN constraint'. Decision is correct (owner wants strict; 3.0 major lifts the 2.x constraint) — but the ADRs weren't amended, so the doc set is internally inconsistent, and P2.2 leans on the old D4 byte-identity reading. FIX: amend ADR 0010 + ADR 0012 D4 to scope byte-identity to 2.x and record the 3.0 strict flip; reword PRD 'shared with the default posture'; have P2.1/P2.4 declare the supersession; reconcile P2.2 wording.

OWNER DECISION:
2. New export get_url_verdicts() (P2.3) not authorized by ADR 0006 (enumerates exactly 3 companion helpers). Amend ADR 0006 for a 4th, or fold verdicts into get_url_diagnostics() columns.

WORDING/SCOPE FIXES (agent-doable):
3. P2.3 'dependency-error -> ok' byte-identity claim overstated (verified: .derive_parse_status_vec maps empty tld/domain to warning-*, not ok). Scope the guarantee.
4. P5.3 '158/99' headline DRIFT-WIDEN: figure is rfc3986-grammar ACCEPTANCE over the 257-row audited fixture (NEWS.md:271-274), not the FSSS-level conformance headline P5.3 elevates it to (tension with P5.3's own FSSS-only claim policy). Label it to its real scope.
5. P5.2 mis-citation: 'reconciliation §10 acceptance gate 8' -> gate 8 lives in S8; recon §10 has no numbered gates. Cite S8 gate 8 / recon §7 G4.
6. Code hygiene (not a record blocker): R/zzz.R:55-56 stale inline comment says caches 'unbounded' (contradicted by 100000L at :77-81); fold inline-comment scan into P5.1's doc-consistency gate.

GROUNDED (no action): P0.4, P1.1, P3.1, P5.1 fully clean; the posture/prepend/admission/credential cluster (highest drift risk) came back clean — http-prepend correctly the single shared scheme_policy=infer seam, browser=repair posture, credential preservation = intended behavior with leakage guarded at output surfaces.

comments.author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com
comments.content:
RESOLVED — all fixes applied + independently verified (2026-07-23).

Applied across 7 branches: new PR #217 (atomic ADR 0010 + ADR 0012 D4 + browser-PRD v3.0 amendment trio) + edits on #210/#211/#212/#213/#215/#216. Verification pass: 8/8 PASS, no new contradiction, no cross-file conflict.

- #1 strict-default supersession now RECORDED (not silent): ADR 0010 + ADR 0012 D4 + browser PRD carry v3.0 amendment blocks; P2.1/P2.4 declare the supersession. Doc set no longer says two contradictory things about the v3 default. D4 amendment enumerates ALL non-scheme_policy constraints as unchanged.
- #2 get_url_verdicts() -> get_parse_verdicts() (disambiguated from the classifier verdict); defined at url_standard=NULL; get_url_diagnostics() shape preserved; ADR 0006 amended to authorize the 4th helper (never-widen preserved).
- #3 dependency-error->ok re-scoped (v3 policy choice, not byte-identity claim); byte-identity guarantee scoped to inputs whose PSL lookup runs.
- #4 P5.3 158/99 re-scoped to rfc3986-grammar acceptance over the 257-row fixture (NEWS.md:271-274), not the FSSS conformance headline.
- #5 P5.2 §10-gate-8 mis-citation fixed. #6 P5.1 gate now scans inline R/** comments (R/zzz.R:55-56).

Every branch: validate-records + validate-transitions PASS; validate-manifest by-design red (unreferenced PROPOSED files) until the owner seal. Block cleared — P-tier merge batch is now 9 PRs (#209-#216 + #217). Marking done; the merge itself is the owner's pending act (tracked on epic RURL-dorofzmb).
